differential_diagnoses: Cushing's syndrome (ectopic or adrenal-driven) leading to opportunistic infection
next_steps: Initiate empiric treatment for fungal and bacterial meningitis (e.g., amphotericin B, flucytosine, and vancomycin/ceftriaxone)
Perform cryptococcal antigen testing in serum and CSF
Perform India ink staining and fungal culture of CSF
Perform acid-fast bacilli staining and culture of CSF
Obtain 24-hour urine free cortisol and plasma ACTH to confirm hypercortisolism
Obtain serum electrolytes and endocrine workup for adrenal function
Consult neurosurgery for evaluation of potential ventriculostomy due to severe intracranial hypertension

differential_diagnoses: Cushing's syndrome secondary to adrenocortical carcinoma
Nocardiosis with central nervous system dissemination
next_steps: Perform cryptococcal antigen testing in serum and CSF
Send CSF for India ink staining, fungal/bacterial/AFB cultures, and cytology
Measure 24-hour urine free cortisol, plasma ACTH, and midnight salivary cortisol
Measure plasma metanephrines to exclude pheochromocytoma
Perform Strongyloides serology and stool ova and parasite exam
Consult neurosurgery for management of increased intracranial pressure
Initiate empiric treatment with liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine, plus broad-spectrum antibiotics (vancomycin, ceftriaxone, ampicillin) pending results
